name: io-ts (Runtime Type Checking)

performance.notes: Runtime validation; minimal overhead for typical schemas

agent_readiness.notes: Runtime type system for TypeScript using fp-ts Either. Define codecs that validate and decode unknown data into typed values. Functional programming oriented: uses fp-ts for error handling. t.string, t.number, t.type(), t.union(), t.array(). decode() returns Either<Errors, T>. Distinguishes validation from type-casting. Powerful but verbose compared to Zod. io-ts v3 (experimental) rewrites with improved API. Preferred by FP teams using fp-ts.

gotchas: Steep learning curve — requires fp-ts familiarity
Verbose compared to Zod for common cases
Error messages less readable than Zod
v3 is experimental and breaking from v2
best_when: When Either<Error, Result> error handling is preferred over exceptions
Strict boundary validation with functional error handling
avoid_when: Teams unfamiliar with functional programming patterns
Simple validation (Zod or ArkType simpler and more ergonomic)
summary: io-ts provides runtime type checking for TypeScript with an fp-ts Either-based API. Define codecs that validate unknown data into typed values. Powerful and correct but verbose — requires fp-ts familiarity. Preferred by functional programming teams using the Either monad for error handling. Zod or ArkType are simpler alternatives for teams not committed to fp-ts patterns.
